key_insight: MF-PhiBE bridges continuous-time mean-field control and discrete-time data by replacing unknown infinitesimal dynamics with one-step estimators in a Wasserstein-space PDE.
engineering_relevance: It enables model-free actor-critic control for systems with interacting populations when only sampled transition data are available.
method: Build a Mean-Field-PhiBE that preserves the McKean-Vlasov HJB generator structure.
Estimate drift/covariance from one-step transitions instead of requiring identifiable continuous-time coefficients.
Combine a policy-gradient theorem for entropy-regularized randomized policies with an actor-critic update.
evidence_signal: The paper proves first-order consistency in general and second-order accuracy in the linear-quadratic case, with numerical tests on LQR and a crowd-aversion example.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality of one-step transition estimates, and finite-sample or discretization noise may affect the PDE-based approximation.
paper_title: Mean-Field PhiBE: Continuous-Time Mean-Field Reinforcement Learning from Discrete-Time Data
